architecture.summary: The system augments a human user's ability to design RL agents for traffic control by using ChatGPT as a knowledge assistant. A user (novice or expert) interacts with ChatGPT via natural language to formulate state spaces and reward functions for a Markov Decision Process (MDP) in mixed traffic control environments. ChatGPT's responses are then used to construct and train an RL agent in one of three traffic simulation environments (ring road, bottleneck, intersection). The flow is: user query → ChatGPT reasoning/suggestion → user implements MDP components → RL training → policy evaluation.

biblio.title: Can ChatGPT Enable ITS? The Case of Mixed Traffic Control via Reinforcement Learning

claims.statement: ChatGPT increases number of successful policies by 150% for intersection compared to solely beginner capabilities
ChatGPT increases number of successful policies by 136% for bottleneck compared to solely beginner capabilities
Some ChatGPT-assisted policies outperform expert-designed policies in intersection and bottleneck scenarios
ChatGPT does not provide consistent improvements across all scenarios

claims.dataset: user study with 70 participants across ring road, bottleneck, and intersection environments
claims.statement: Study evaluated whether novices can leverage ChatGPT to solve complex mixed traffic control problems through developing state space and reward function formulations
